items.name: Gravcraft transportation
items.overview:
# Gravcraft Systems

### Reactionless Gravitic Vehicles of Chartered Space

> "Out on the frontier, gravcraft are as common as pickup trucks once were. They don't defy gravity so much as negotiate with it."

---

## Overview

Gravcraft are reactionless vehicles employing compact gravitic field generators to manipulate local spacetime gradients. Unlike aircraft, gravcraft do not rely upon aerodynamic lift, rotor systems, or thrust-vector propulsion for low-altitude operation. Instead, they generate a controlled counter-gravitic field that reduces effective mass and creates directional acceleration through spacetime gradient manipulation.

In practical terms, a grav vehicle hovers silently, moves in any direction, and requires little infrastructure beyond a suitable landing zone.

Within the Hostile setting, gravcraft remain expensive, maintenance-intensive technologies largely confined to government agencies, major corporations, military forces, and wealthy colonial administrations. Most frontier settlements continue to rely upon wheeled, tracked, or VTOL vehicles due to lower costs and easier maintenance.

A gravcraft is often regarded as a symbol of authority, wealth, or off-world influence.

---

# Technology Background

## The H³ Catalytic Grav Core

The gravitic revolution began with the development of the Helium-3 Catalytic Singularity Reactor (H3-CSR).

Using advanced fusion techniques, a grav core converts Helium-3 fusion products into ultra-high-density energy capable of sustaining localized spacetime distortions.

The core powers a set of gravitic projectors arranged throughout the vehicle's structure.

These projectors create a controlled "mass attenuation envelope" around the vehicle.

Rather than eliminating gravity, the envelope reduces the vehicle's effective interaction with local gravity wells by up to 98%.

This dramatically lowers the energy required for lift and acceleration.

---

## Zero-Point Extraction Matrix

Higher-end military and corporate vehicles supplement fusion reactors with Zero-Point Extraction Matrices (ZPEMs).

These devices exploit quantum vacuum fluctuations to provide low-level continuous power.

ZPEMs cannot power a gravcraft independently but greatly extend operational endurance by reducing fuel consumption.

In frontier regions, functioning ZPEMs are highly prized and frequently salvaged from derelict military or corporate vessels.

---

## Operational Characteristics

### Advantages

- Silent hover capability
- No runways required
- Excellent rough-terrain mobility
- Low atmospheric disturbance
- Capable of operating over water, ice, swamps, and broken terrain
- Minimal moving parts

### Disadvantages

- Expensive grav-core maintenance
- Helium-3 fuel dependency
- Susceptible to EMP and gravitic interference
- Difficult to repair in isolated colonies
- Field generators degrade rapidly in dusty environments

---

## Frontier Reality

On core worlds, gravcraft are common.

On frontier worlds, they are rare.

A mining colony may possess only a handful:

- Governor's transport
- Corporate survey craft
- Colonial marshal vehicle
- Emergency medevac

A working grav vehicle is often one of the most valuable assets in an entire settlement.

---

---

# Using Gravcraft in a Hostile Campaign

For a frontier campaign featuring Wolfric Reven, gravcraft should feel rare and valuable rather than ubiquitous.

A typical independent colonial marshal might own:

- One aging Roadrunner grav bike
- A refurbished Pathfinder survey craft
- A modified Mule utility skimmer

Such vehicles become part transport, part home, and part livelihood—requiring fuel contracts, spare parts, favors from mechanics, and occasional scavenging expeditions to keep them operational. This preserves the grounded, blue-collar tone of Hostile while allowing the cinematic freedom associated with Firefly, The Mandalorian, and Cowboy Bebop.

items.name: Surface Vehicle Technologies
items.overview:
# Surface Vehicle Technologies of Chartered Space

### Transportation on the Frontier

While gravcraft capture the imagination of colonists and media holos, they represent only a tiny fraction of vehicles operating beyond the Core Worlds. Most frontier settlers depend upon simpler, cheaper, and more reliable transportation technologies that can be maintained with local parts and limited technical expertise.

A Colonial Marshal may arrive in a grav skimmer, but the rancher, miner, surveyor, and trader are more likely to travel by wheel, track, rotor, or wing.

In the Hostile universe, practical transportation always wins over elegant transportation.

# Vehicle Availability in a Typical Hostile Frontier Colony

Vehicle TypeAvailabilityWheeled Utility VehiclesVery CommonCargo TrucksVery CommonTracked VehiclesCommonHovercraftCommon on suitable worldsRotorcraftCommonVTOL AircraftUncommonFixed-Wing AircraftUncommonGrav BikesRareGrav SkimmersRareCorporate Grav VehiclesVery RareMilitary Grav VehiclesExtremely Rare

This distribution preserves the blue-collar, industrial feel of Hostile. Most colonists still travel in noisy trucks, worn-out crawlers, and aging VTOLs. Gravcraft exist, but like private jets or helicopters in the 20th century, they remain symbols of wealth, authority, or strategic importance. A lone marshal arriving on a grav bike still turns heads wherever he lands.

items.name: Tracked Vehicles
items.overview:
# Tracked Vehicles

## Overview

When terrain becomes impossible for wheels, tracks take over.

Tracked vehicles dominate:

- Arctic colonies
- Mining worlds
- Volcanic regions
- Swamps
- Mountain operations

Tracks sacrifice speed for mobility and durability.

---

## Technology

Most tracked vehicles employ:

- Hydrogen-electric drives
- Fuel-cell systems
- Hybrid fusion generators

items.name: VTOL Aircraft
items.overview:
# VTOL Aircraft

## Overview

Vertical Takeoff and Landing aircraft fill the gap between aircraft and spacecraft.

Most colonies possess at least one VTOL because they can operate without prepared runways.

VTOLs are often more practical than gravcraft and significantly cheaper.

---

## Technology

Common propulsion systems include:

- Ducted fans
- Tilt rotors
- Lift jets
- Fusion-electric turbofans

Modern VTOLs use sophisticated flight computers to manage stability.

---

## Advantages

- High speed
- Long range
- Easy maintenance
- Excellent cargo capacity

---

## Disadvantages

- Loud
- Fuel hungry
- Weather dependent

---

###

items.name: Wheeled Vehicles
items.overview:
# Wheeled Vehicles

## Overview

Wheeled vehicles remain the most common form of transportation on settled worlds.

Simple, rugged, and easily repaired, wheeled vehicles can be manufactured locally using industrial equipment found in most colonies.

Unlike gravcraft, they require no advanced field generators, fusion systems, or exotic components.

A mechanic with basic tools can keep a wheeled vehicle operating for decades.

---

## Technology

Power systems vary:

- Hydrogen fuel cells
- Internal combustion engines
- Electric batteries
- Compact fusion generators

Most frontier vehicles use hydrogen fuel cells due to their reliability and the availability of hydrogen from local water sources.

---

## Common Uses

- Cargo hauling
- Agriculture
- Mining operations
- Long-distance travel
- Colonial security patrols

---

###

items.name: Colonial Rangers
items.overview:
**Role:** Frontier Law Enforcement

**First Look:** Extrasolar Colonization Administration (ESCA) 

**Callsign:** Colonial Rangers

# Colonial Ranger Division (CRD) 

> ## The Frontier Law Enforcement program
>
> Commissioned by the **Extrasolar Colonization Administration** (ESCA) under the **United Corporate Combine** (UCC) 

Humanity expanded too quickly.

Hundreds of colonial worlds were settled during the Expansion Rush using fusion-age jump corridors and corporate-sponsored migration fleets. Many colonies failed. Some vanished. Others survived only through isolation, local militias, and barter economies.

An agreement of independent colonies with the consent of all three alliances to support joint funding for remote colonial health care, law enforcement and judicial court system. 

This is practiced similar to third world relief funds, non-government organizational aid programs, and unpoliced international oceans.

- Privateer crews will take up this banner to fight pirates and receive stipends, bounties and free annual maintenance at participating starbases and shipyards.
- Colonial Ranger program provides retired or surplus military gear to itinerant law enforcers travelling from colony to colony serving justice and transporting prisoners due trial in larger courts. 

The UCC—the loose interstellar governance body maintaining interstellar law—lacks the ships and manpower to police the frontier directly.

Instead, it licenses independent Rangers.

These Rangers:

- Enforce interstellar law
- Investigate piracy and smuggling
- Mediate colonial disputes
- Track fugitives
- Escort cargo
- Recover lost survey teams
- Investigate alien threats
- Maintain diplomatic contact with isolated settlements

Wolfric Reven once served within the Colonial Security Bureau, but after a failed operation involving corporate corruption and civilian casualties, he resigned his commission rather than become an enforcer for political interests.

Now he operates independently under a Colonial Ranger Charter.
items.revealed:
## The Colonial Rangers (ESCA Frontier Service)

**Official Designation:** Colonial Ranger Division (CRD)\
**Nickname:** Rangers, Long Riders, Dust Marshals, Star Rangers\
**Parent Organization:** European Space Colonization Authority

### Mission

The Colonial Rangers exist to maintain law and order in settlements too remote, poor, or dangerous to justify a permanent ESCA security presence.

Most frontier worlds have:

- No standing military
- No police force larger than a few deputies
- Communication delays measured in days or weeks
- Vast wilderness beyond settled zones

The Rangers fill the gap.

When colonists say:

*"Someone should do something about it."*

The answer is usually:

*"Send for a Ranger."*

---

## Historical Origins

The Colonial Rangers were created after several disastrous colonial incidents:

- Mining settlements seized by corporate mercenaries
- Pirate raids against isolated colonies
- Colonial governors operating as dictators
- Labor uprisings that local authorities could not contain
- Disappearances along newly established trade routes

ESCA realized that large military deployments were impractical.

Instead, they adopted an older model:

A small number of highly competent officers empowered to act independently across enormous territories.

Like the historical Rangers, their effectiveness depends more on reputation than numbers.

---

## Jurisdiction

A single Ranger may be responsible for:

- Multiple star systems
- Hundreds of settlements
- Millions of square kilometers of wilderness
- Dozens of orbital stations

One Ranger's territory might take months to patrol completely.

---

## Authority

A Colonial Ranger carries an ESCA Commission granting authority to:

- Investigate crimes
- Arrest fugitives
- Conduct frontier inspections
- Mediate colonial disputes
- Enforce ESCA regulations
- Deputize civilians when necessary

Their authority often exceeds that of local officials.

This creates tension with:

- Colonial governors
- Corporate security forces
- Mining consortiums
- Settlement councils

> • Wu-Ketai Trust and Banking • **Nørsk Cargo Line** • Wu-Ketai Industries • Wu-Ketai Powertech • **Wu-Ketai Mining** • Wu-Ketai Aerospace • Bauspace Brandings • McConnell Aerospace • Stern Weapon Technologies • Novomo Retail • Wu-Ketai Steel Mills • **Makita Genetics** 

Joseph Wu is one of the Earth’s few billionaires and the owner of Wu-Ketai, a huge and profi table corporation that has many related companies. Although Wu retains complete ownership and control of Wu-Ketai, the commercial set-up of companies within it is varied and complex. Each of the companies operating under the Wu-Ketai brand is a separate entity, with Joseph Wu completely owning some and holding minority or majority stakes in others. In some instances, he has simply licensed the brand to a company that has purchased a division from him. The Wu-Ketai brand is strongest in manufacturing, with companies like Wu-Ketai Industries, Wu-Ketai Power Tech, Wu-Ketai Mining and Wu-Ketai Aerospace creating huge profi ts. Joseph Wu has spread the corporation into mining, colonial sponsorship, entertainment, food production, publishing and automobiles. 

Scenario Seed: **Makita Genetics**, the conglomerate’s successful genetics corporation, has been supplying the United States military with bioweapon prototypes for the past fi ve years. These bioweapons are an attempt by the American superpower to ‘equalize’ the Tau Ceti war and strike at invisible and undetectable enemy infi ltrators. The dye-markers, bioluminescent algae and genetically-tailored viruses have so far been ineff ectual, but the Pentagon is convinced that victory might just lay with some type of weaponized biology – and not bombs. Should the corporation succeed then the unrestricted corporate development license for Tau Ceti has been promised to Wu-Ketai!

Planetology: The Epsilon Eridani system is dominated by a three gas giant planets, three lifeless and uninhabited airless planets and ‘The Belts’. These asteroid belts are located at 3 and 20 astronomical units (AU) away from the parent star, respectively. A gas giant named Ægir, which is roughly Jupiter-sized, orbits just beyond the Inner Belt at a distance of 3.5 AU from the star.

Development: Many of the bigger asteroids in the Inner Belt have attracted resource scouts, miners and colonists. These asteroids are known as ‘The Solomons’ after the mythical mines of Biblical King Solomon; all are densely populated hives of activity – they serve as starports, cities, meeting points, mineral processing centres and entertainment complexes. In order of population and importance, the largest of the Soloman asteroids are Central, Tasker, Incubu, Suliman and Payback. In contravention of Interstellar Commerce Organization rules that state a single world should come under a single governing entity, the Inner Belt is a corporate and political mess. Most of the big corporations have bases in the Epsilon Eridani system, and each of the Solomons is governed by one of those corporate giants. Thus the star system is balkanized and serves almost as a free-trade area, where competitors rub shoulders with one another, deals are struck and both corporate espionage and terrorism are attempted.

items.name: United Corporate Combine (UCC)
items.overview: In 2170 the major corporations of the world came together in Singapore to create the United Corporate Combine - essentially a standing inter-corporate council. The Combine’s first task was to establish agencies which would regulate international business and practices, something the United Nations had once done, and that the Earth Union had then continued. Corporations now seemed to wield the most power on and off of Earth, and their ‘voters’ were their customers. Nations relied on these industrial giants for both their valuable business and their lucrative investments.

items.overview:
**Role:** Frontier Law Enforcement

**First Look:** Extrasolar Colonization Administration (ESCA) 

**Callsign:** Colonial Rangers

# Colonial Ranger Division (CRD) 

> ## The Frontier Law Enforcement program
>
> Commissioned by the **Extrasolar Colonization Administration** (ESCA) under the **United Corporate Combine** (UCC) 

Humanity expanded too quickly.

Hundreds of colonial worlds were settled during the Expansion Rush using fusion-age jump corridors and corporate-sponsored migration fleets. Many colonies failed. Some vanished. Others survived only through isolation, local militias, and barter economies.

An agreement of independent colonies with the consent of all three alliances to support joint funding for remote colonial health care, law enforcement and judicial court system. 

This is practiced similar to third world relief funds, non-government organizational aid programs, and unpoliced international oceans.

- Privateer crews will take up this banner to fight pirates and receive stipends, bounties and free annual maintenance at participating starbases and shipyards.
- Colonial Ranger program provides retired or surplus military gear to itinerant law enforcers travelling from colony to colony serving justice and transporting prisoners due trial in larger courts. 

The UCC—the loose interstellar governance body maintaining interstellar law—lacks the ships and manpower to police the frontier directly.

Instead, it licenses independent Rangers.

These Rangers:

- Enforce interstellar law
- Investigate piracy and smuggling
- Mediate colonial disputes
- Track fugitives
- Escort cargo
- Recover lost survey teams
- Investigate alien threats
- Maintain diplomatic contact with isolated settlements

Wolfric Reven once served within the Colonial Security Bureau, but after a failed operation involving corporate corruption and civilian casualties, he resigned his commission rather than become an enforcer for political interests.

Now he operates independently under a Colonial Ranger Charter.

items.name: Gravcraft transportation
items.overview:
# Gravcraft Systems

### Reactionless Gravitic Vehicles of Chartered Space

> "Out on the frontier, gravcraft are as common as pickup trucks once were. They don't defy gravity so much as negotiate with it."

---

## Overview

Gravcraft are reactionless vehicles employing compact gravitic field generators to manipulate local spacetime gradients. Unlike aircraft, gravcraft do not rely upon aerodynamic lift, rotor systems, or thrust-vector propulsion for low-altitude operation. Instead, they generate a controlled counter-gravitic field that reduces effective mass and creates directional acceleration through spacetime gradient manipulation.

In practical terms, a grav vehicle hovers silently, moves in any direction, and requires little infrastructure beyond a suitable landing zone.

Within the Hostile setting, gravcraft remain expensive, maintenance-intensive technologies largely confined to government agencies, major corporations, military forces, and wealthy colonial administrations. Most frontier settlements continue to rely upon wheeled, tracked, or VTOL vehicles due to lower costs and easier maintenance.

A gravcraft is often regarded as a symbol of authority, wealth, or off-world influence.

---

# Technology Background

## The H³ Catalytic Grav Core

The gravitic revolution began with the development of the Helium-3 Catalytic Singularity Reactor (H3-CSR).

Using advanced fusion techniques, a grav core converts Helium-3 fusion products into ultra-high-density energy capable of sustaining localized spacetime distortions.

The core powers a set of gravitic projectors arranged throughout the vehicle's structure.

These projectors create a controlled "mass attenuation envelope" around the vehicle.

Rather than eliminating gravity, the envelope reduces the vehicle's effective interaction with local gravity wells by up to 98%.

This dramatically lowers the energy required for lift and acceleration.

---

## Zero-Point Extraction Matrix

Higher-end military and corporate vehicles supplement fusion reactors with Zero-Point Extraction Matrices (ZPEMs).

These devices exploit quantum vacuum fluctuations to provide low-level continuous power.

ZPEMs cannot power a gravcraft independently but greatly extend operational endurance by reducing fuel consumption.

In frontier regions, functioning ZPEMs are highly prized and frequently salvaged from derelict military or corporate vessels.

---

## Operational Characteristics

### Advantages

- Silent hover capability
- No runways required
- Excellent rough-terrain mobility
- Low atmospheric disturbance
- Capable of operating over water, ice, swamps, and broken terrain
- Minimal moving parts

### Disadvantages

- Expensive grav-core maintenance
- Helium-3 fuel dependency
- Susceptible to EMP and gravitic interference
- Difficult to repair in isolated colonies
- Field generators degrade rapidly in dusty environments

---

## Frontier Reality

On core worlds, gravcraft are common.

On frontier worlds, they are rare.

A mining colony may possess only a handful:

- Governor's transport
- Corporate survey craft
- Colonial marshal vehicle
- Emergency medevac

A working grav vehicle is often one of the most valuable assets in an entire settlement.

---

---

# Using Gravcraft in a Hostile Campaign

For a frontier campaign featuring Wolfric Reven, gravcraft should feel rare and valuable rather than ubiquitous.

A typical independent colonial marshal might own:

- One aging Roadrunner grav bike
- A refurbished Pathfinder survey craft
- A modified Mule utility skimmer

Such vehicles become part transport, part home, and part livelihood—requiring fuel contracts, spare parts, favors from mechanics, and occasional scavenging expeditions to keep them operational. This preserves the grounded, blue-collar tone of Hostile while allowing the cinematic freedom associated with Firefly, The Mandalorian, and Cowboy Bebop.

items.overview:
## The Colonial Rangers (ESCA Frontier Service)

**Official Designation:** Colonial Ranger Division (CRD)\
**Nickname:** Rangers, Long Riders, Dust Marshals, Star Rangers\
**Parent Organization:** European Space Colonization Authority

### Mission

The Colonial Rangers exist to maintain law and order in settlements too remote, poor, or dangerous to justify a permanent ESCA security presence.

Most frontier worlds have:

- No standing military
- No police force larger than a few deputies
- Communication delays measured in days or weeks
- Vast wilderness beyond settled zones

The Rangers fill the gap.

When colonists say:

*"Someone should do something about it."*

The answer is usually:

*"Send for a Ranger."*

---

## Historical Origins

The Colonial Rangers were created after several disastrous colonial incidents:

- Mining settlements seized by corporate mercenaries
- Pirate raids against isolated colonies
- Colonial governors operating as dictators
- Labor uprisings that local authorities could not contain
- Disappearances along newly established trade routes

ESCA realized that large military deployments were impractical.

Instead, they adopted an older model:

A small number of highly competent officers empowered to act independently across enormous territories.

Like the historical Rangers, their effectiveness depends more on reputation than numbers.

---

## Jurisdiction

A single Ranger may be responsible for:

- Multiple star systems
- Hundreds of settlements
- Millions of square kilometers of wilderness
- Dozens of orbital stations

One Ranger's territory might take months to patrol completely.

---

## Authority

A Colonial Ranger carries an ESCA Commission granting authority to:

- Investigate crimes
- Arrest fugitives
- Conduct frontier inspections
- Mediate colonial disputes
- Enforce ESCA regulations
- Deputize civilians when necessary

Their authority often exceeds that of local officials.

This creates tension with:

- Colonial governors
- Corporate security forces
- Mining consortiums
- Settlement councils

items.name: Gravcraft Transportation
items.overview:
# Gravcraft Systems

### Reactionless Gravitic Vehicles of Chartered Space

> "Out on the frontier, gravcraft are as common as pickup trucks once were. They don't defy gravity so much as negotiate with it."

---

## Overview

Gravcraft are reactionless vehicles employing compact gravitic field generators to manipulate local spacetime gradients. Unlike aircraft, gravcraft do not rely upon aerodynamic lift, rotor systems, or thrust-vector propulsion for low-altitude operation. Instead, they generate a controlled counter-gravitic field that reduces effective mass and creates directional acceleration through spacetime gradient manipulation.

In practical terms, a grav vehicle hovers silently, moves in any direction, and requires little infrastructure beyond a suitable landing zone.

Within the Hostile setting, gravcraft remain expensive, maintenance-intensive technologies largely confined to government agencies, major corporations, military forces, and wealthy colonial administrations. Most frontier settlements continue to rely upon wheeled, tracked, or VTOL vehicles due to lower costs and easier maintenance.

A gravcraft is often regarded as a symbol of authority, wealth, or off-world influence.

---

# Technology Background

## The H³ Catalytic Grav Core

The gravitic revolution began with the development of the Helium-3 Catalytic Singularity Reactor (H3-CSR).

Using advanced fusion techniques, a grav core converts Helium-3 fusion products into ultra-high-density energy capable of sustaining localized spacetime distortions.

The core powers a set of gravitic projectors arranged throughout the vehicle's structure.

These projectors create a controlled "mass attenuation envelope" around the vehicle.

Rather than eliminating gravity, the envelope reduces the vehicle's effective interaction with local gravity wells by up to 98%.

This dramatically lowers the energy required for lift and acceleration.

---

## Zero-Point Extraction Matrix

Higher-end military and corporate vehicles supplement fusion reactors with Zero-Point Extraction Matrices (ZPEMs).

These devices exploit quantum vacuum fluctuations to provide low-level continuous power.

ZPEMs cannot power a gravcraft independently but greatly extend operational endurance by reducing fuel consumption.

In frontier regions, functioning ZPEMs are highly prized and frequently salvaged from derelict military or corporate vessels.

---

## Operational Characteristics

### Advantages

- Silent hover capability
- No runways required
- Excellent rough-terrain mobility
- Low atmospheric disturbance
- Capable of operating over water, ice, swamps, and broken terrain
- Minimal moving parts

### Disadvantages

- Expensive grav-core maintenance
- Helium-3 fuel dependency
- Susceptible to EMP and gravitic interference
- Difficult to repair in isolated colonies
- Field generators degrade rapidly in dusty environments

---

## Frontier Reality

On core worlds, gravcraft are common.

On frontier worlds, they are rare.

A mining colony may possess only a handful:

- Governor's transport
- Corporate survey craft
- Colonial marshal vehicle
- Emergency medevac

A working grav vehicle is often one of the most valuable assets in an entire settlement.

---

# Using Gravcraft in a Hostile Campaign

For a frontier campaign, gravcraft should feel rare and valuable rather than ubiquitous.

A typical independent colonial ranger might own:

- One aging Roadrunner grav bike
- A refurbished Pathfinder survey craft
- A modified Mule utility skimmer

Such vehicles become part transport, part home, and part livelihood—requiring fuel contracts, spare parts, favors from mechanics, and occasional scavenging expeditions to keep them operational. This preserves the grounded, blue-collar tone of Hostile while allowing the cinematic freedom associated with Firefly, The Mandalorian, and Cowboy Bebop.

items.name: Surface Vehicle Technologies
items.overview:
# Surface Vehicle Technologies of Chartered Space

### Transportation on the Frontier

While gravcraft capture the imagination of colonists and media holos, they represent only a tiny fraction of vehicles operating beyond the Core Worlds. Most frontier settlers depend upon simpler, cheaper, and more reliable transportation technologies that can be maintained with local parts and limited technical expertise.

A Colonial Marshal may arrive in a grav skimmer, but the rancher, miner, surveyor, and trader are more likely to travel by wheel, track, rotor, or wing.

In the Hostile universe, practical transportation always wins over elegant transportation.

# Vehicle Availability in a Typical Hostile Frontier Colony

Vehicle TypeAvailabilityWheeled Utility VehiclesVery CommonCargo TrucksVery CommonTracked VehiclesCommonHovercraftCommon on suitable worldsRotorcraftCommonVTOL AircraftUncommonFixed-Wing AircraftUncommonGrav BikesRareGrav SkimmersRareCorporate Grav VehiclesVery RareMilitary Grav VehiclesExtremely Rare

This distribution preserves the blue-collar, industrial feel of Hostile. Most colonists still travel in noisy trucks, worn-out crawlers, and aging VTOLs. Gravcraft exist, but like private jets or helicopters in the 20th century, they remain symbols of wealth, authority, or strategic importance. A lone marshal arriving on a grav bike still turns heads wherever he lands.

# VTOL Aircraft

## Overview

Vertical Takeoff and Landing aircraft fill the gap between aircraft and spacecraft.

Most colonies possess at least one VTOL because they can operate without prepared runways.

VTOLs are often more practical than gravcraft and significantly cheaper.

---

## Technology

Common propulsion systems include:

- Ducted fans
- Tilt rotors
- Lift jets
- Fusion-electric turbofans

Modern VTOLs use sophisticated flight computers to manage stability.

---

## Advantages

- High speed
- Long range
- Easy maintenance
- Excellent cargo capacity

---

## Disadvantages

- Loud
- Fuel hungry
- Weather dependent

---

###

items.name: Wheeled Vehicles
items.overview:
# Wheeled Vehicles

## Overview

Wheeled vehicles remain the most common form of transportation on settled worlds.

Simple, rugged, and easily repaired, wheeled vehicles can be manufactured locally using industrial equipment found in most colonies.

Unlike gravcraft, they require no advanced field generators, fusion systems, or exotic components.

A mechanic with basic tools can keep a wheeled vehicle operating for decades.

---

## Technology

Power systems vary:

- Hydrogen fuel cells
- Internal combustion engines
- Electric batteries
- Compact fusion generators

Most frontier vehicles use hydrogen fuel cells due to their reliability and the availability of hydrogen from local water sources.

---

## Common Uses

- Cargo hauling
- Agriculture
- Mining operations
- Long-distance travel
- Colonial security patrols

---

items.overview:
Headquarters: Hong Kong Main 

Regional Offices: Cold Mountain (Luna), New York, Miami, Chicago, Houston, Beijing, Xian, Shanghai, Mumbai, London, Sydney. 

Chairman: Joseph Wu 

Related Businesses 

> • Wu-Ketai Trust and Banking • **Nørsk Cargo Line** • Wu-Ketai Industries • Wu-Ketai Powertech • **Wu-Ketai Mining** • Wu-Ketai Aerospace • Bauspace Brandings • McConnell Aerospace • Stern Weapon Technologies • Novomo Retail • Wu-Ketai Steel Mills • **Makita Genetics** 

Joseph Wu is one of the Earth’s few billionaires and the owner of Wu-Ketai, a huge and profi table corporation that has many related companies. Although Wu retains complete ownership and control of Wu-Ketai, the commercial set-up of companies within it is varied and complex. Each of the companies operating under the Wu-Ketai brand is a separate entity, with Joseph Wu completely owning some and holding minority or majority stakes in others. In some instances, he has simply licensed the brand to a company that has purchased a division from him. The Wu-Ketai brand is strongest in manufacturing, with companies like Wu-Ketai Industries, Wu-Ketai Power Tech, Wu-Ketai Mining and Wu-Ketai Aerospace creating huge profi ts. Joseph Wu has spread the corporation into mining, colonial sponsorship, entertainment, food production, publishing and automobiles. 

Scenario Seed: **Makita Genetics**, the conglomerate’s successful genetics corporation, has been supplying the United States military with bioweapon prototypes for the past fi ve years. These bioweapons are an attempt by the American superpower to ‘equalize’ the Tau Ceti war and strike at invisible and undetectable enemy infi ltrators. The dye-markers, bioluminescent algae and genetically-tailored viruses have so far been ineff ectual, but the Pentagon is convinced that victory might just lay with some type of weaponized biology – and not bombs. Should the corporation succeed then the unrestricted corporate development license for Tau Ceti has been promised to Wu-Ketai!

items.overview:
THE SOLOMONS NEZ 0808 A000675 USSC 

Frozen/Asteroid, Non-Ind, Non-Agri G 

Epsilon Eridani K2V 

Planetology: The Epsilon Eridani system is dominated by a three gas giant planets, three lifeless and uninhabited airless planets and ‘The Belts’. These asteroid belts are located at 3 and 20 astronomical units (AU) away from the parent star, respectively. A gas giant named Ægir, which is roughly Jupiter-sized, orbits just beyond the Inner Belt at a distance of 3.5 AU from the star.

Development: Many of the bigger asteroids in the Inner Belt have attracted resource scouts, miners and colonists. These asteroids are known as ‘The Solomons’ after the mythical mines of Biblical King Solomon; all are densely populated hives of activity – they serve as starports, cities, meeting points, mineral processing centres and entertainment complexes. In order of population and importance, the largest of the Soloman asteroids are Central, Tasker, Incubu, Suliman and Payback. In contravention of Interstellar Commerce Organization rules that state a single world should come under a single governing entity, the Inner Belt is a corporate and political mess. Most of the big corporations have bases in the Epsilon Eridani system, and each of the Solomons is governed by one of those corporate giants. Thus the star system is balkanized and serves almost as a free-trade area, where competitors rub shoulders with one another, deals are struck and both corporate espionage and terrorism are attempted.

**Role:** Frontier Law Enforcement

**First Look:** Extrasolar Colonization Administration (ESCA) 

**Callsign:** Colonial Rangers

# Colonial Ranger Division (CRD) 

> ## The Frontier Law Enforcement program
>
> Commissioned by the **Extrasolar Colonization Administration** (ESCA) under the **United Corporate Combine** (UCC) 

Humanity expanded too quickly.

Hundreds of colonial worlds were settled during the Expansion Rush using fusion-age jump corridors and corporate-sponsored migration fleets. Many colonies failed. Some vanished. Others survived only through isolation, local militias, and barter economies.

An agreement of independent colonies with the consent of all three alliances to support joint funding for remote colonial health care, law enforcement and judicial court system. 

This is practiced similar to third world relief funds, non-government organizational aid programs, and unpoliced international oceans.

- Privateer crews will take up this banner to fight pirates and receive stipends, bounties and free annual maintenance at participating starbases and shipyards.
- Colonial Ranger program provides retired or surplus military gear to itinerant law enforcers travelling from colony to colony serving justice and transporting prisoners due trial in larger courts. 

The UCC—the loose interstellar governance body maintaining interstellar law—lacks the ships and manpower to police the frontier directly.

Instead, it licenses independent Rangers.

These Rangers:

- Enforce interstellar law
- Investigate piracy and smuggling
- Mediate colonial disputes
- Track fugitives
- Escort cargo
- Recover lost survey teams
- Investigate alien threats
- Maintain diplomatic contact with isolated settlements

Wolfric Reven once served within the Colonial Security Bureau, but after a failed operation involving corporate corruption and civilian casualties, he resigned his commission rather than become an enforcer for political interests.

Now he operates independently under a Colonial Ranger Charter.

items.name: Gravcraft transportation
items.overview:
# Gravcraft Systems

### Reactionless Gravitic Vehicles of Chartered Space

> "Out on the frontier, gravcraft are as common as pickup trucks once were. They don't defy gravity so much as negotiate with it."

---

## Overview

Gravcraft are reactionless vehicles employing compact gravitic field generators to manipulate local spacetime gradients. Unlike aircraft, gravcraft do not rely upon aerodynamic lift, rotor systems, or thrust-vector propulsion for low-altitude operation. Instead, they generate a controlled counter-gravitic field that reduces effective mass and creates directional acceleration through spacetime gradient manipulation.

In practical terms, a grav vehicle hovers silently, moves in any direction, and requires little infrastructure beyond a suitable landing zone.

Within the Hostile setting, gravcraft remain expensive, maintenance-intensive technologies largely confined to government agencies, major corporations, military forces, and wealthy colonial administrations. Most frontier settlements continue to rely upon wheeled, tracked, or VTOL vehicles due to lower costs and easier maintenance.

A gravcraft is often regarded as a symbol of authority, wealth, or off-world influence.

---

# Technology Background

## The H³ Catalytic Grav Core

The gravitic revolution began with the development of the Helium-3 Catalytic Singularity Reactor (H3-CSR).

Using advanced fusion techniques, a grav core converts Helium-3 fusion products into ultra-high-density energy capable of sustaining localized spacetime distortions.

The core powers a set of gravitic projectors arranged throughout the vehicle's structure.

These projectors create a controlled "mass attenuation envelope" around the vehicle.

Rather than eliminating gravity, the envelope reduces the vehicle's effective interaction with local gravity wells by up to 98%.

This dramatically lowers the energy required for lift and acceleration.

---

## Zero-Point Extraction Matrix

Higher-end military and corporate vehicles supplement fusion reactors with Zero-Point Extraction Matrices (ZPEMs).

These devices exploit quantum vacuum fluctuations to provide low-level continuous power.

ZPEMs cannot power a gravcraft independently but greatly extend operational endurance by reducing fuel consumption.

In frontier regions, functioning ZPEMs are highly prized and frequently salvaged from derelict military or corporate vessels.

---

## Operational Characteristics

### Advantages

- Silent hover capability
- No runways required
- Excellent rough-terrain mobility
- Low atmospheric disturbance
- Capable of operating over water, ice, swamps, and broken terrain
- Minimal moving parts

### Disadvantages

- Expensive grav-core maintenance
- Helium-3 fuel dependency
- Susceptible to EMP and gravitic interference
- Difficult to repair in isolated colonies
- Field generators degrade rapidly in dusty environments

---

## Frontier Reality

On core worlds, gravcraft are common.

On frontier worlds, they are rare.

A mining colony may possess only a handful:

- Governor's transport
- Corporate survey craft
- Colonial marshal vehicle
- Emergency medevac

A working grav vehicle is often one of the most valuable assets in an entire settlement.

# Using Gravcraft in a Hostile Campaign

For a frontier campaign featuring Wolfric Reven, gravcraft should feel rare and valuable rather than ubiquitous.

A typical independent colonial marshal might own:

- One aging Roadrunner grav bike
- A refurbished Pathfinder survey craft
- A modified Mule utility skimmer

Such vehicles become part transport, part home, and part livelihood—requiring fuel contracts, spare parts, favors from mechanics, and occasional scavenging expeditions to keep them operational. This preserves the grounded, blue-collar tone of Hostile while allowing the cinematic freedom associated with Firefly, The Mandalorian, and Cowboy Bebop.

items.name: Colonial Ranger Division (CRD)
items.overview:
## The Colonial Rangers (ESCA Frontier Service)

**Official Designation:** Colonial Ranger Division (CRD)\
**Nickname:** Rangers, Long Riders, Dust Marshals, Star Rangers\
**Parent Organization:** European Space Colonization Authority

### Mission

The Colonial Rangers exist to maintain law and order in settlements too remote, poor, or dangerous to justify a permanent ESCA security presence.

Most frontier worlds have:

- No standing military
- No police force larger than a few deputies
- Communication delays measured in days or weeks
- Vast wilderness beyond settled zones

The Rangers fill the gap.

When colonists say:

*"Someone should do something about it."*

The answer is usually:

*"Send for a Ranger."*

---

## Historical Origins

The Colonial Rangers were created after several disastrous colonial incidents:

- Mining settlements seized by corporate mercenaries
- Pirate raids against isolated colonies
- Colonial governors operating as dictators
- Labor uprisings that local authorities could not contain
- Disappearances along newly established trade routes

ESCA realized that large military deployments were impractical.

Instead, they adopted an older model:

A small number of highly competent officers empowered to act independently across enormous territories.

Like the historical Rangers, their effectiveness depends more on reputation than numbers.

---

## Jurisdiction

A single Ranger may be responsible for:

- Multiple star systems
- Hundreds of settlements
- Millions of square kilometers of wilderness
- Dozens of orbital stations

One Ranger's territory might take months to patrol completely.

---

## Authority

A Colonial Ranger carries an ESCA Commission granting authority to:

- Investigate crimes
- Arrest fugitives
- Conduct frontier inspections
- Mediate colonial disputes
- Enforce ESCA regulations
- Deputize civilians when necessary

Their authority often exceeds that of local officials.

This creates tension with:

- Colonial governors
- Corporate security forces
- Mining consortiums
- Settlement councils

items.name: Surface Vehicle Technologies
items.overview:
# Surface Vehicle Technologies of Chartered Space

### Transportation on the Frontier

While gravcraft capture the imagination of colonists and media holos, they represent only a tiny fraction of vehicles operating beyond the Core Worlds. Most frontier settlers depend upon simpler, cheaper, and more reliable transportation technologies that can be maintained with local parts and limited technical expertise.

A Colonial Marshal may arrive in a grav skimmer, but the rancher, miner, surveyor, and trader are more likely to travel by wheel, track, rotor, or wing.

In the Hostile universe, practical transportation always wins over elegant transportation.

# Vehicle Availability in a Typical Hostile Frontier Colony

Vehicle TypeAvailabilityWheeled Utility VehiclesVery CommonCargo TrucksVery CommonTracked VehiclesCommonHovercraftCommon on suitable worldsRotorcraftCommonVTOL AircraftUncommonFixed-Wing AircraftUncommonGrav BikesRareGrav SkimmersRareCorporate Grav VehiclesVery RareMilitary Grav VehiclesExtremely Rare

This distribution preserves the blue-collar, industrial feel of Hostile. Most colonists still travel in noisy trucks, worn-out crawlers, and aging VTOLs. Gravcraft exist, but like private jets or helicopters in the 20th century, they remain symbols of wealth, authority, or strategic importance. A lone marshal arriving on a grav bike still turns heads wherever he lands.

items.name: United Corporate Combine (UCC)
items.overview: In 2170 the major corporations of the world came together in Singapore to create the United Corporate Combine - essentially a standing inter-corporate council. The Combine’s first task was to establish agencies which would regulate international business and practices, something the United Nations had once done, and that the Earth Union had then continued. The Earth Union had ceased to exist in all but name two years earlier at the height of the First Recession. Corporations now seemed to wield the most power on and off of Earth, and their ‘voters’ were their customers. Nations relied on these industrial giants for both their valuable business and their lucrative investments.

items.name: VTOL Aircraft
items.overview:
# VTOL Aircraft

## Overview

Vertical Takeoff and Landing aircraft fill the gap between aircraft and spacecraft.

Most colonies possess at least one VTOL because they can operate without prepared runways.

VTOLs are often more practical than gravcraft and significantly cheaper.

---

## Technology

Common propulsion systems include:

- Ducted fans
- Tilt rotors
- Lift jets
- Fusion-electric turbofans

Modern VTOLs use sophisticated flight computers to manage stability.

---

## Advantages

- High speed
- Long range
- Easy maintenance
- Excellent cargo capacity

---

## Disadvantages

- Loud
- Fuel hungry
- Weather dependent

---

###

items.name: Wheeled Vehicles
items.overview:
# Wheeled Vehicles

## Overview

Wheeled vehicles remain the most common form of transportation on settled worlds.

Simple, rugged, and easily repaired, wheeled vehicles can be manufactured locally using industrial equipment found in most colonies.

Unlike gravcraft, they require no advanced field generators, fusion systems, or exotic components.

A mechanic with basic tools can keep a wheeled vehicle operating for decades.

---

## Technology

Power systems vary:

- Hydrogen fuel cells
- Internal combustion engines
- Electric batteries
- Compact fusion generators

Most frontier vehicles use hydrogen fuel cells due to their reliability and the availability of hydrogen from local water sources.

---

## Common Uses

- Cargo hauling
- Agriculture
- Mining operations
- Long-distance travel
- Colonial security patrols

---

items.name: Wu-Ketai  - [Faction / Location]
items.overview:
Headquarters: Hong Kong Main 

Regional Offices: Cold Mountain (Luna), New York, Miami, Chicago, Houston, Beijing, Xian, Shanghai, Mumbai, London, Sydney. 

Chairman: Joseph Wu 

Related Businesses 

> • Wu-Ketai Trust and Banking • **Nørsk Cargo Line** • Wu-Ketai Industries • Wu-Ketai Powertech • **Wu-Ketai Mining** • Wu-Ketai Aerospace • Bauspace Brandings • McConnell Aerospace • Stern Weapon Technologies • Novomo Retail • Wu-Ketai Steel Mills • **Makita Genetics** 

Joseph Wu is one of the Earth’s few billionaires and the owner of Wu-Ketai, a huge and profi table corporation that has many related companies. Although Wu retains complete ownership and control of Wu-Ketai, the commercial set-up of companies within it is varied and complex. Each of the companies operating under the Wu-Ketai brand is a separate entity, with Joseph Wu completely owning some and holding minority or majority stakes in others. In some instances, he has simply licensed the brand to a company that has purchased a division from him. The Wu-Ketai brand is strongest in manufacturing, with companies like Wu-Ketai Industries, Wu-Ketai Power Tech, Wu-Ketai Mining and Wu-Ketai Aerospace creating huge profi ts. Joseph Wu has spread the corporation into mining, colonial sponsorship, entertainment, food production, publishing and automobiles. 

Scenario Seed: **Makita Genetics**, the conglomerate’s successful genetics corporation, has been supplying the United States military with bioweapon prototypes for the past fi ve years. These bioweapons are an attempt by the American superpower to ‘equalize’ the Tau Ceti war and strike at invisible and undetectable enemy infi ltrators. The dye-markers, bioluminescent algae and genetically-tailored viruses have so far been ineff ectual, but the Pentagon is convinced that victory might just lay with some type of weaponized biology – and not bombs. Should the corporation succeed then the unrestricted corporate development license for Tau Ceti has been promised to Wu-Ketai!

docs.title: 5PFH Campaign Turn Sequence
docs.text:
**Daily Life Steps**
- Update status (injuries, cargo, heat, rivals, favors, reputation).
- Assign/resolve crew tasks (@[Core p.77|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#79]).
- Determine job offers (@[Core p.83|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#85]).
- Check for Rivals (@[Core p.85|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#87]).
- Select your Job (Patron, Rival, Quest, Salvage, etc.).
- Resolve Rumors (@[Core p.85|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#87]).
- Resolve Mission Steps or Merchant Mission Steps.
- Story Event/Track if turn without one (@[Core p.66|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#68], @[Core p.153|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#155]).

**Mission Steps**
1. Determine the objective (may use Expanded Missions, @[p.74|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#76])
1. Mission Selection (Salvage Jobs)
1. Faction Involvement* (unknown during selection; could be a surprise) (@[CBH p.110|5PFH 3e Compendium/Bug Hunt#110])
1. Check for Connections (@[p.164|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#166], or Expanded Connection @[CBH p.80|5PFH 3e Compendium/Bug Hunt#80] – Opportunity mission only)
1. Assign equipment (@[p.85|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#87])
1. Deployment (@[pp.88-94|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#90]) — see Deployment considerations below
1. Battle (go to tabletop)
1. Resolve Tabletop Post-battle activities — see Post-battle activities below

**Deployment considerations** (step 6)
- Check deployment conditions (not Salvage)
- Determine notable sights (not Salvage)
- Determine the enemy (@[p.92|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#94])
- Set up the battlefield (@[p.108|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#108]; Terrain Generation, @[CBH 94|5PFH 3e Compendium/Bug Hunt#94])
- Set up Points of interest and Salvage (Salvage mission only)

**Post-battle activities** (step 8)
- a. Resolve Rival status (@[p.119|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#121])
- b. Check for illegal Psionic usage (@[p.21|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#23])
- c. Resolve Patron status (@[p.119|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#121])
- d. Roll quest progress (@[p.120|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#122], @[CBH p.78|5PFH 3e Compendium/Bug Hunt#78])
- e. Get paid (@[p.120|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#122])
- f. Trade Salvage (@[p.147|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#147] — Salvage mission only)
- g. Battlefield finds (@[p.121|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#123])
- h. Check for Salvage mission points of interest (@[CBH p.139|5PFH 3e Compendium/Bug Hunt#139] — Salvage mission only)
- i. Check for World Event Steps
- j. Check for Settlement Event Steps
- k. Gather the loot (@[p.121|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#123], @[p.131+|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#133])
- l. Determine injuries and recovery (@[p.121|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#123])
- m. EXP and character upgrades (@[p.123|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#125])
- n. Invest in advanced training (@[p.124|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#126])
- o. Purchase items (@[p.125|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#127])
- p. Roll for a character event (@[p.126|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#128], @[p.128|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#130])
- q. Resolve Heat check

**Travel Steps**
- Flee invasion (@[Core p.69|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#71]).
- Check for Factions fleeing (@[CBH p.114|5PFH 3e Compendium/Bug Hunt#114]).
- Decide whether to travel (@[Core p.69|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#71]).
- Resolve steps, Local Travel / Starship Travel (@[Core p.70|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#72]), as applicable.
- Resolve New World Arrival steps (@[Core p.72|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#74])
- Check for Shipping Issues (Starship, Planetary, Overland).
- Check for Settlement Event Steps.

**Settlement Event Steps**
- Check for invasion (@[Core p.69|5PFH Five Parsecs From Home v3#71]).
- Check for Instability (@[CBH p.148|5PFH 3e Compendium/Bug Hunt#148]).
- Roll a Negotiations check.
- Check for Faction Conflict
- Check for Psionic legality status (@[CBH p.20|5PFH 3e Compendium/Bug Hunt#20])
